title: Legal commentary podcast
explanation:
Legal commentary podcast

Listen to a lawyer discussing a court ruling.

Transcript

Host: Did the ruling surprise you?
Guest: Not entirely. The judges emphasised that freedom of expression is not absolute and must be balanced against privacy rights. They did not create new law; they applied existing principles to digital platforms. However, the dissenting opinion warned that the decision could chill legitimate journalism.

questions.text: Freedom of expression must be balanced with [*privacy rights|traffic laws|tax rules].
The court [*applied existing principles|created entirely new law|ignored all precedent].
The dissenting opinion feared the ruling could [*chill journalism|increase profits|end the internet].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The case concerns digital ____.  | *platforms | plants | planes
The judges said free speech is not ____.  | *absolute | available | ancient
A dissenting ____ was mentioned. | *opinion | option | opera
The guest speaks as a ____.  | *lawyer | chef | driver
The ruling relates to [*balancing rights|sports funding|cooking recipes].
The guest suggests the decision was [*moderate in scope|revolutionary|irrelevant].
Journalists might self-censor if they fear [*legal consequences|bad weather|high prices].
